conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture on Correspondence Chromatic Number of Planar Graphs Without Cycles 4–8
paper_title: Correspondence coloring and its application to list-coloring planar graphs without cycles of lengths 4 to 8
summary: Jin, Kang, and Zhu (arXiv:2412.19059, December 2024) proved the stronger Theorem 1.4: every planar graph with no cycle of length 4, 6, or 8 is DP-3-colorable. Since any planar graph without cycles of lengths 4 to 8 is a special case (having no 4-, 6-, or 8-cycles), the conjecture of Dvořák and Postle follows as an immediate corollary. The paper explicitly identifies this as resolving the conjecture from arXiv:1508.03437, describing it as a ‘more natural and stronger claim’ that such graphs are DP-3-colorable without the consistency assumption.

since_posted.title: Planar graphs having no cycle of length 4, 6 or 8 are DP-3-colorable
since_posted.authors: Ligang Jin, Yingli Kang, Xuding Zhu
since_posted.claim: Proves that every planar graph with no cycle of length 4, 6, or 8 is DP-3-colorable (Theorem 1.4), which is strictly stronger than the conjecture and implies it as a corollary; the paper explicitly cites the Dvořák–Postle conjecture from 1508.03437.

notes: The conjecture is resolved as a corollary of a strictly stronger theorem proved in arXiv:2412.19059. The stronger result only requires excluding cycles of lengths 4, 6, and 8 (not all five lengths 4–8), making it a genuine strengthening rather than merely a proof of the original statement.
